phenotypes.label: Pituitary tumor with hypopituitarism
phenotypes.description: Patients with pituitary neoplasm and downstream anterior pituitary failure, reflected by panhypopituitarism and episodes of diabetes insipidus. Associated endocrine and systemic features include metabolic syndrome, perimenopausal symptoms, fatigue, and occasional neurologic signs such as incoordination.

phenotypes.label: Mixed aneurysm and functional symptoms
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine arterial and intracranial aneurysms (including subarachnoid hemorrhage) with functional neurologic/conversion symptoms, abnormal liver enzymes, esophageal stricture with dysphagia, and herpes zoster complications. There is no shared clinical theme across these findings, reflecting unrelated systems rather than a single condition.

phenotypes.label: BPH with lower urinary tract symptoms
phenotypes.description: Cluster of male lower urinary tract disease centered on benign prostatic hyperplasia with outlet obstruction. Patients show nocturia, urinary frequency, incomplete emptying and retention, with raised PSA and bladder neck obstruction; erectile dysfunction and testicular hypofunction commonly co-occur in this age group.

phenotypes.label: Solid malignancy with masses and lymphadenopathy
phenotypes.description: Patients with primary solid malignancies—including bone and soft‑tissue tumors—presenting with localized masses or lumps, pelvic lymphadenopathy, and lung nodules/masses. Associated findings include platelet abnormalities with petechiae and bleeding diathesis, and neck pain/cervicogenic headaches suggestive of skeletal or cervical involvement. Reflux esophagitis and perineal/genital infections appear as frequent co‑occurring conditions in this group.

phenotypes.label: Melanoma, lung and pancreatic neoplasms
phenotypes.description: Patients carry solid-organ cancers with tumor-related imaging findings. Prominent conditions include cutaneous melanoma (including face/scalp/trunk), lung cancer with abnormal chest imaging and pulmonary nodules, and pancreatic cysts and neoplasms; brain metastasis and GI carcinoids also appear. Overall this reflects oncologic disease across multiple organs.

phenotypes.label: Autoimmune hypothyroidism and comorbidities
phenotypes.description: Acquired hypothyroidism dominated by Hashimoto thyroiditis, with autoimmune thyroiditis features. Co-occurring autoimmune disorders (celiac disease, pernicious anemia, polymyalgia rheumatica/systemic sclerosis) and nutrient deficiencies (vitamin B12 and vitamin D) are frequent, along with osteoporosis and fatigue/malaise.

phenotypes.label: PTSD/depression, substance use, dental disease
phenotypes.description: Patients show a behavioral health profile dominated by PTSD, anxiety, and recurrent major depression, with frequent alcohol, nicotine, and opioid dependence. Oral health problems are enriched, including dental caries, periodontitis, retained roots, and tooth loss. This reflects a cohort with psychiatric illness and substance use disorders coupled with significant dental disease.

phenotypes.label: Unused low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions mix common chronic comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, obesity, diabetes) with scattered, unrelated problems such as central scotoma, retinal vein occlusion, brain injury, and skin carcinoma in situ. There is no unifying clinical theme or organ system, so this cluster is not clinically interpretable.

phenotypes.label: ADHD and childhood emotional disorders
phenotypes.description: Patients exhibit ADHD—predominately inattentive subtype—often alongside childhood emotional disorders and attention/motor control difficulties. Comorbid anxiety, dysthymia, agoraphobia, and sleep disturbances are frequent; occasional autonomic or endocrine findings (e.g., postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome, low testosterone) appear but are secondary.

phenotypes.label: Unused low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions reflect ubiquitous musculoskeletal pain and degenerative spine complaints (e.g., low back and neck pain, radiculopathy, spinal stenosis). These are broadly distributed across patients and do not define a distinct, selectively identifying clinical pattern here.

phenotypes.label: GI bloating and stroke sequelae
phenotypes.description: Prominent features are abdominal distension, flatulence, burping, and abnormal stool findings, consistent with a functional gastrointestinal gas and bloating presentation. The same records also include vascular and cerebrovascular disease with post stroke cognitive and balance deficits, along with dryness symptoms and stress. These reflect unrelated clinical themes rather than a single unified condition.

phenotypes.label: Sickle cell and valvular mix
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between a sickle cell disease cluster—acute chest syndrome, avascular necrosis of the femoral head, functional asplenia, and iron overload—and a non‑rheumatic valvular heart disease cluster with pulmonary hypertension/cor pulmonale (mitral, aortic, and tricuspid valve disorders). These represent distinct mechanisms and patient groups rather than a single clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Ocular disease and nephrolithiasis
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions cluster into two unrelated groups: retinal and macular disorders (age-related macular degeneration, retinal neovascularization, central serous chorioretinopathy) and nephrolithiasis with calcium metabolism abnormalities (kidney/ureteral stones, hypercalciuria, hyperoxaluria). Additional immune-related diagnoses (hypogammaglobulinemia, immune thrombocytopenia, pneumocystosis) and mucocutaneous lichenoid disease further indicate disparate themes consolidated together.

phenotypes.label: Embolic stroke with PFO/ASD
phenotypes.description: Cluster centers on acute cerebrovascular ischemia and its sequelae, including cerebral infarction, TIA, aphasia, hemiparesis, and other late effects of stroke. Co-occurrence of atrial septal defect/ostium secundum and patent foramen ovale points to paradoxical embolic mechanisms, with gynecologic malignancy suggesting a hypercoagulable contribution to stroke risk.

phenotypes.label: Mixed stroke, fungal, head-neck cancer
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split across unrelated themes: acute cerebrovascular disease with hemiplegia, endemic/opportunistic fungal infections with pulmonary involvement, and head and neck malignancies. Additional elements (latent syphilis/high-risk sexual behavior and hip tendinopathies) further diversify the pattern. There is no single clinical theme uniting these conditions.

phenotypes.label: Female breast cancer and risk
phenotypes.description: Female breast malignancies spanning in situ and invasive disease, with quadrant-specific diagnoses and estrogen receptor–positive/negative status, alongside abnormal mammography, palpable breast lumps, and calcifications. It also reflects hereditary breast cancer risk and postsurgical states after mastectomy/reconstruction, including acquired absence of breast, absent nipple, deformity of reconstructed breast, and lymphedema.

phenotypes.label: Varicose veins and stasis ulcers
phenotypes.description: Chronic venous disease of the lower extremities, centered on venous insufficiency and varicose veins, with edema/lymphedema and leg pain. The pattern includes stasis dermatitis and chronic lower‑leg ulcers, with complications such as superficial phlebitis, deep venous thrombosis, thromboembolism, and cellulitis.

phenotypes.label: Glaucoma, cataract, and vitreoretinal disease
phenotypes.description: Patients carry chronic ophthalmic conditions centered on primary open‑angle glaucoma and age-related cataracts, often with ocular hypertension, dry eye, and glaucoma suspect diagnoses. Related vitreoretinal and inflammatory disorders co-occur, including vitreous degeneration, epiretinal membrane, cystoid macular changes, uveitis/iridocyclitis, retinal vein occlusion, and optic atrophy, with visual disturbance or impairment noted.

phenotypes.label: Pregnancy and obstetric complications
phenotypes.description: Pregnancy-focused cluster spanning normal and high‑risk pregnancies across all trimesters. Includes hypertensive disorders of pregnancy, UTIs in pregnancy, threatened preterm labor, placental abnormalities, fetal growth restriction, hyperemesis, RhD negativity, maternal obesity, uterine scar, and conditions affecting labor and delivery.

phenotypes.label: Epilepsy and B cell lymphomas
phenotypes.description: This combines two unrelated clusters: focal epilepsy and seizure disorders alongside multiple non‑Hodgkin B‑cell lymphoma subtypes. Additional neurologic features such as Parkinson’s disease, tremor, REM sleep behavior disorder, and cognitive changes appear, but there is no single unifying condition tying the lymphoma findings to the seizure/epilepsy group.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are generic chronic comorbidities and nonspecific symptoms (hypertension, type 2 diabetes, GERD, obesity, pain) alongside scattered unrelated diagnoses (e.g., seizure-related terms), without a unifying clinical theme. This reflects a baseline mix seen across many patients rather than a cohesive condition cluster.

phenotypes.label: Bipolar disorder with hyperparathyroidism
phenotypes.description: Co-occurrence of bipolar spectrum disorders (including manic and hypomanic episodes) with primary hyperparathyroidism and hypercalcemia. Associated findings include thyroid nodular disease and stone-related diagnoses (cholelithiasis, sialolithiasis), with occasional scarring dermatoses, indicating an endocrine–neuropsychiatric comorbidity pattern.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: No single clinical pattern ties these conditions together. The leading terms mix substance-related mental health crises, respiratory complications, metabolic/electrolyte disturbances, and nonspecific pain without a unifying story.

phenotypes.label: Peripheral neuropathy and mononeuropathies
phenotypes.description: Pattern centers on disorders of the peripheral nervous system: polyneuropathy, idiopathic peripheral neuropathy, and focal mononeuropathies such as carpal and tarsal tunnel syndromes. Musculoskeletal complaints and general comorbidities (GERD, thyroid disease, hypertension) co-occur but are secondary to the neuropathy theme.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: Leading conditions are generic primary-care complaints and comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, GERD, back pain, mood and GI symptoms) mixed with scattered unrelated diagnoses. There is no shared clinical theme across them, so this does not define a usable condition cluster.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are generic chronic comorbidities and common symptoms without a unifying clinical theme. This reflects general background health issues rather than a specific disease cluster.

phenotypes.label: Diabetic retinopathy and erectile dysfunction
phenotypes.description: Advanced diabetic eye disease is prominent, with macular edema, proliferative/nonproliferative diabetic retinopathy, vitreous hemorrhage, retinal artery occlusion, and cataract alongside refractive and ocular surface disorders. Erectile dysfunction and male hypogonadism co-occur, consistent with microvascular and endocrine complications seen in diabetes; occasional central lesions (e.g., cerebral cyst, hypopituitarism) also appear.

phenotypes.label: Ocular disease and pericarditis
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between extensive ophthalmic pathology (retinal detachment/tear, keratoconus and other corneal disorders, pseudophakia/intraocular lens, diabetic retinopathy, strabismus, and blindness) and cardiac pericardial disease (acute pericarditis, pericardial effusion). These represent unrelated organ systems without a unifying mechanism, indicating a merged set of patients rather than a single clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Neurogenic bladder with recurrent UTIs
phenotypes.description: Pattern consistent with neurogenic bladder and recurrent urinary tract infections, often in the context of spinal cord injury or spina bifida with chronic catheterization. Associated features include urinary retention, hydronephrosis, hematuria, bladder stones, pyelonephritis, Escherichia coli infection, and occasional sepsis. Co-occurring paraplegia, wheelchair dependence, and pressure ulcers reflect chronic immobility and neurogenic dysfunction.

phenotypes.label: Gastric cancer with metastases
phenotypes.description: Stomach malignancy with metastatic and disseminated involvement. Associated conditions include bone metastases, pleural thickening and effusions (including loculated), pericardial effusion, intra-abdominal lymphadenopathy, pulmonary nodules or pneumothorax, bowel obstruction, and hepatomegaly, reflecting advanced spread and complications. Acquired absence of organ codes suggest prior resection status in some patients.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between gastrointestinal neuroendocrine/carcinoid tumors (small intestine primaries with liver metastases) and inherited lipid and coagulation disorders (familial hypercholesterolemia, hyperlipoproteinemia, hereditary thrombophilia/protein S deficiency). Incisional abdominal wall hernias and nonspecific intra‑abdominal masses add further heterogeneity. These are unrelated processes rather than a single clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Low-signal comorbidity baseline
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are common chronic cardiometabolic comorbidities such as hypertension, dyslipidemia, type 2 diabetes, coronary disease, atrial fibrillation, and heart failure, with associated CKD and obesity. This reflects a broad baseline rather than a specific, delineated condition cluster useful for targeted cohort selection.

phenotypes.label: Cirrhosis with portal hypertension complications
phenotypes.description: Advanced chronic liver disease with cirrhosis and portal hypertension. Findings include ascites, esophageal/gastric varices, splenomegaly with thrombocytopenia or pancytopenia, hepatic encephalopathy, portal vein thrombosis, spontaneous bacterial peritonitis, hepatic failure, and hepatocellular carcinoma; some patients have a history of liver transplant. Etiologies represented include chronic hepatitis C, alcohol-related disease, and nonalcoholic steatohepatitis or steatosis.

phenotypes.label: Voice disorders with transplant and leukemia
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions center on laryngeal and voice pathology such as dysphonia, vocal cord paralysis, laryngeal edema/spasm, and dysphagia. These are intermixed with heart transplant diagnoses (presence, rejection) and hematologic disorders (acute myeloid leukemia, remission, von Willebrand disease), which are unrelated conditions without a unifying clinical story.

phenotypes.label: Rheumatoid arthritis with VTE and osteoporosis
phenotypes.description: Rheumatoid arthritis dominates, including seropositive and seronegative disease with multi‑joint involvement, synovitis, and erosive arthropathy. There is a strong venous thromboembolism signal (deep vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism, including infarction) alongside osteoporosis with vertebral compression fractures. Autoimmune‑associated features such as ocular inflammation/dry eye, mouth ulcers, and alopecia areata are also represented.

phenotypes.label: Multiple sclerosis and ADHD/autism
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between demyelinating and cranial nerve disorders (multiple sclerosis, optic neuritis, facial palsy) and neurodevelopmental/behavioral diagnoses (ADHD, autism). These represent different patient groups and do not form a single coherent clinical pattern.

phenotypes.label: Distal limb musculoskeletal disorders
phenotypes.description: Musculoskeletal conditions of the distal limbs, with prominence of foot and ankle pain, plantar fascia disorders, tendinitis/enthesopathy, flatfoot, hallux valgus, and ankle sprain. Wrist and hand problems also appear, including carpal and forearm fractures, carpal tunnel syndrome, and hand osteoarthritis. The pattern reflects overuse, degenerative change, and acute injuries affecting feet and hands.

phenotypes.label: HIV and herpesvirus infections
phenotypes.description: HIV infection with prominent herpesvirus disease, including herpes simplex and vesicular dermatitis. Co-occurring infections and sequelae (e.g., typhoid fever, reactive arthritis), ocular/skin infections, and hepatic or genitourinary findings (jaundice, disordered bilirubin excretion, persistent hematuria) appear, alongside substance use disorders common in this population.

phenotypes.label: Hematologic malignancy with transplant/GVHD complications
phenotypes.description: Pattern of leukemia and Hodgkin lymphoma with history of bone marrow transplant, complicated by graft-versus-host disease and treatment-related cytopenias. Frequent infectious complications are present, especially gastrointestinal infections and viral enteritis, including cytomegalovirus; epistaxis aligns with thrombocytopenia. Ocular findings (macular scarring, cataract) and rashes also co-occur in this immunocompromised group.

phenotypes.label: Corpus baseline blend
phenotypes.description: Top conditions are common chronic comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes) and nonspecific symptoms (chest pain, abdominal pain, nausea) seen broadly across patients. No shared clinical theme ties these findings together, so this is not a distinct condition pattern.

phenotypes.label: Primary brain tumor with complications
phenotypes.description: Neuro-oncology pattern centered on primary brain malignancy with mass effect and focal deficits. Findings include headaches, abnormal brain imaging, cerebral edema, obstructive hydrocephalus, optic disc edema, hemiplegia, and postoperative states. Head and neck involvement (including tonsillar cancer) appears in the same cluster, consistent with head–neck oncologic presentations overlapping CNS disease.

phenotypes.label: Bronchiectasis/CF and pituitary disorders
phenotypes.description: Two distinct patterns are present. One centers on bronchiectasis and cystic fibrosis with cough, dyspnea, hypoxemia, pancreatic insufficiency, and nontuberculous mycobacterial infection. The other centers on pituitary adenomas with hyperprolactinemia and related pituitary dysfunction; these are unrelated pulmonary and endocrine themes merged together.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are a broad mix of routine chronic comorbidities (hypertension, GERD, hyperlipidemia, obesity, diabetes) alongside common symptoms and mood disorders. There is no unifying clinical mechanism or organ system focus, reflecting a general primary‑care baseline rather than a distinct phenotype.

phenotypes.label: COPD, HIV, male genital disorders
phenotypes.description: Two unrelated clusters dominate: chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, and a set of male genital tract conditions (epididymitis, hydrocele, varicocele, phimosis) occurring alongside HIV/immunodeficiency and CMV. These represent separate organ-system themes with no single unifying clinical mechanism.

phenotypes.label: Recurrent major depression with anxiety
phenotypes.description: Patients have recurrent major depressive disorder—most often mild to moderate—with codes for active episodes and remission, alongside generalized anxiety disorder and other anxiety states. This reflects mood–anxiety comorbidity and a chronic, recurrent course. Other unrelated conditions appear sporadically and are not central.

phenotypes.label: Ulcerative colitis and rectal bleeding
phenotypes.description: Ulcerative colitis predominates, spanning pancolitis, proctitis, and rectosigmoiditis with complications such as rectal hemorrhage, hematochezia, melena, pseudopolyposis, and post-surgical pouchitis. Presentations feature diarrhea and anorectal disorders consistent with active inflammatory bowel disease.

phenotypes.label: Uterine/cervical cancer and hysterectomy
phenotypes.description: Endometrial and cervical cancers with related premalignant lesions (endometrial hyperplasia/EIN) predominate. Post-hysterectomy status and radiation-related sequelae, plus nodal involvement and postoperative states, indicate gynecologic cancer history and follow-up.

phenotypes.label: Knee osteoarthritis and meniscal derangement
phenotypes.description: Degenerative knee disease with osteoarthritis, knee pain, effusion, and internal derangements such as meniscal tears and chondromalacia. Common comorbidities like hypertension, obesity, and GERD appear but the primary pattern is knee pathology.

phenotypes.label: IBD with perianal disease
phenotypes.description: Inflammatory bowel disease features prominently, especially Crohn’s and ulcerative colitis with marked perianal involvement (perianal/rectal abscesses, anal fistula, proctitis). Associated conditions include malabsorption, anorectal disorders, rectal polyps, and postoperative gastrointestinal sequelae, consistent with complicated IBD courses.

phenotypes.label: Myasthenia gravis
phenotypes.description: This cluster is anchored by myasthenia gravis, an autoimmune neuromuscular junction disorder causing fluctuating skeletal muscle weakness. Ocular and bulbar features are prominent, with diplopia, facial weakness, dysarthria, and dysphagia appearing among associated neurologic findings.

phenotypes.label: Photodamaged skin and age-related eye disease
phenotypes.description: Cluster of sun-exposed skin disease with pigmented lesions (actinic keratoses, lentigines, seborrheic keratoses, melanocytic nevi) and cutaneous malignancies (basal and squamous cell carcinoma). Co-occurring age-related ocular findings (cataracts, vitreous changes, presbyopia/astigmatism) suggest older adults with chronic photodamage.

phenotypes.label: Graves disease with thyrotoxicosis
phenotypes.description: Autoimmune hyperthyroidism centered on Graves disease, with thyrotoxicosis and diffuse toxic goiter, often accompanied by ophthalmopathy (exophthalmos). Some patients show hypercoagulability or antiphospholipid syndrome and cardiovascular involvement; post‑ablative hypothyroidism appears in this cluster as well.

phenotypes.label: Shoulder disorders, Crohn’s disease, lung cancer
phenotypes.description: This combines three unrelated themes: degenerative/rotator cuff shoulder disorders, Crohn’s disease with intestinal complications, and lung malignancy with postsurgical findings (including absence of lung). These represent musculoskeletal, gastrointestinal inflammatory disease, and pulmonary cancer patterns without a single unifying clinical mechanism.

phenotypes.label: Schizophrenia and schizoaffective disorders
phenotypes.description: This theme centers on schizophrenia and schizoaffective disorders, including paranoid and chronic subtypes. Co-occurring conditions include drug-induced dyskinesia, blepharospasm and diplopia, and metabolic abnormalities such as impaired glucose tolerance; ocular findings like borderline open-angle glaucoma also appear.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine ocular disorders (vitreous opacities, retinal and optic nerve disease, cataract) alongside sarcoidosis, which often has ocular involvement. However, prominent sexual activity status entries and anal dysplasia/carcinoma in situ are unrelated to the ocular–sarcoidosis cluster, indicating two disparate clinical stories grouped together.

phenotypes.label: Mixed benign masses and acute conditions
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine benign breast disease and other cystic lesions with cranial nerve tumors (acoustic neuroma) and sudden hearing loss, alongside acute gastrointestinal and hepatic diagnoses. Additional entries include appendicitis, gastritis, viral meningitis, Guillain–Barré syndrome, patellar fracture, and hematologic malignancy. These represent unrelated organ systems without a single unifying clinical mechanism.

phenotypes.label: Dementia and bipolar disorders
phenotypes.description: Neurocognitive disorders dominate, including Alzheimer’s disease, vascular dementia, and dementia with behavioral disturbance, with associated findings of impaired cognition and visual hallucinations. Bipolar spectrum disorders, predominantly depressive episodes, co-occur, suggesting a dementia cohort with significant mood comorbidity. Cerebrovascular events (embolic stroke) and arrhythmia (paroxysmal supraventricular tachycardia) appear alongside, consistent with older patients with vascular contributions to cognitive decline.

phenotypes.label: Peripheral neuropathies with foot-drop
phenotypes.description: This cluster reflects neuromuscular and peripheral nerve disorders, including inflammatory/toxic neuropathies, common peroneal nerve lesion, and foot-drop under a myoneural disorder umbrella. Concomitant pharyngeal disorders and sleep-disordered breathing (sleep hypoventilation, sleep apnea) point to bulbar and respiratory muscle involvement in some patients. Overall, it represents peripheral neuropathy with lower-limb motor deficits and cranial involvement.

phenotypes.label: Thyroid cancer with post-thyroidectomy sequelae
phenotypes.description: Thyroid malignancy and nodular goiter clustered with post-thyroidectomy sequelae. Patients show postoperative hypothyroidism and hypoparathyroidism with hypocalcemia, with occasional recurrent laryngeal nerve involvement causing dysphonia or vocal cord paralysis.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions mix common mental health diagnoses and nonspecific complaints (anxiety, depression, URI, cough, headache, abdominal and back pain) with unrelated gynecologic issues (menstrual irregularity, vaginitis, uterine fibroids, abnormal Pap/HPV). There is no unifying clinical theme, reflecting baseline complaints rather than a coherent disease cluster.

phenotypes.label: Mixed endocrine, otologic, hematologic disorders
phenotypes.description: Unrelated conditions are grouped together, including adrenal and menopausal disorders (hypercortisolism, postmenopausal bleeding), otologic/vestibular disease (Ménière’s disease, sensorineural hearing loss, tinnitus, vertigo), and hematologic and gastrointestinal findings (polycythemia vera, thalassemia, colon polyps), plus salivary gland disease. These span different organ systems with no shared clinical mechanism, so this does not represent a single coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Psychiatric, skin, and ear disorders
phenotypes.description: Leading conditions mix neuropsychiatric disorders (bipolar II, social phobia, OCD, delayed sleep phase) with dermatologic disease (acne, hidradenitis) and unrelated ear/vestibular and neurologic findings (sudden hearing loss, vertigo, spasmodic torticollis), with scattered endocrine items. There is no single clinical pathway tying these together, so this is not a usable phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Allergic rhinitis, asthma, and chronic sinusitis
phenotypes.description: Atopic airway disease characterized by allergic rhinitis (including pollen, animal dander, and dust mite sensitization) clustered with chronic rhinosinusitis and nasal structural conditions such as turbinate hypertrophy, deviated septum, and nasal polyps. Asthma co-occurs across severities, including IgE‑mediated and eosinophilic phenotypes, with cough and nasal congestion commonly documented.

phenotypes.label: Urogynecologic pelvic floor disorders
phenotypes.description: Female pelvic floor disorders with lower urinary tract symptoms, including stress/urge/mixed urinary incontinence, overactive bladder, frequency, and nocturia, co-occur with pelvic organ prolapse (cystocele, rectocele, uterovaginal prolapse). Atrophic vaginitis and fecal incontinence are associated, suggesting postmenopausal women with pelvic floor laxity and combined urinary and pelvic support symptoms.

phenotypes.label: End-stage kidney disease and transplant
phenotypes.description: Advanced chronic kidney disease with end‑stage renal disease, often with a kidney transplant and transplant-related complications (rejection, failed graft, disorders of the transplanted kidney). Core features include CKD stages 3–5, proteinuria/glomerular disease, hypertensive nephrosclerosis or renal failure, and anemia of chronic kidney disease. Mineral and electrolyte complications are prominent, including secondary hyperparathyroidism, renal osteodystrophy, and hyperkalemia, with immunodeficiency states reflecting post‑transplant status.

phenotypes.label: Sleep-disordered breathing and hypersomnolence
phenotypes.description: Sleep disorders centered on obstructive sleep apnea and central hypersomnolence, with contributions from narcolepsy/cataplexy and sleep-related movement disorders (restless legs, periodic limb movements). Patients commonly have daytime inattention and cognitive complaints alongside headache and migraine, with some showing idiopathic intracranial hypertension or a history of intracranial injury. Disturbances of smell and taste are also enriched in this group.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are common cardiometabolic comorbidities seen broadly in general care rather than a focused disease cluster. Additional scattered items do not form a unified clinical story, so this pattern is not useful for targeted cohort selection.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: Essential tremor and related tremor features appear, but they are accompanied by unrelated diagnoses. The list also includes colon polyps and hemorrhoidal tags, limb fractures and knee ligament sprain, cutaneous melanoma, and hematologic disorders, which do not share a single clinical mechanism. This set does not represent one coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Type 1 diabetes and cancers
phenotypes.description: Two unrelated condition clusters dominate: type 1 diabetes with typical complications (hyper/hypoglycemia, ketoacidosis, neuropathy, retinopathy, nephropathy) and genitourinary malignancy, chiefly prostate cancer, with metastatic spread (bone, lung), cancer pain, and lymphadenopathy. These represent distinct mechanisms and care pathways rather than a single coherent condition set.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: Leading conditions split across unrelated areas: venous thromboembolism and hypercoagulable states (lower-extremity DVT, lupus anticoagulant, prothrombin mutation), HPV-related anogenital/cervical lesions, and facial trauma/infections. Additional items include conjunctival hemorrhage, nontraumatic hemoperitoneum, autoimmune hepatitis, and malabsorption. There is no single unifying clinical mechanism, so this is not a coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Psoriasis with psoriatic arthritis
phenotypes.description: Psoriatic disease with prominent cutaneous psoriasis and joint involvement. Findings include plaque psoriasis with nail changes and associated inflammatory arthritis (psoriatic arthritis/arthropathy, dactylitis, and axial involvement).

phenotypes.label: Sexual health and nail disorders
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between sexual/reproductive health documentation (sexual activity with men, contraception use, Trichomonas) and dermatologic/podiatric problems (onychodystrophy, dermatophytosis, ingrown nail, difficulty walking), with mood and depression screening codes also present. These reflect unrelated care domains rather than a single clinical pattern.

phenotypes.label: Uncomplicated herpes zoster (shingles)
phenotypes.description: Records center on uncomplicated herpes zoster (shingles) diagnoses. These represent localized dermatomal zoster without documented ophthalmic, neurologic, or disseminated complications.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are generic acute symptoms—pain, nausea, chest pain, shortness of breath, vomiting, and diarrhea—mixed with common baseline issues like anxiety and hypertension, without a unifying disease process. This pattern is too nonspecific to support a distinct clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Insomnia and otologic complaints
phenotypes.description: Prominent insomnia and sleep-maintenance disorders co-occur with multiple otologic conditions including sensorineural and asymmetric hearing loss, unilateral hearing loss, tympanic membrane perforation, and dizziness. Dystonia and other scattered neurologic/vascular items are also present. These represent unrelated sleep and ear/neurologic complaints without a single clinical theme tying them together.

phenotypes.label: SLE/Sjögren’s with interstitial lung disease
phenotypes.description: Systemic autoimmune connective tissue disease centered on systemic lupus erythematosus and Sjögren’s syndrome, with Raynaud’s, sicca, alopecia, polyarthropathy, and myositis/overlap features. Prominent respiratory involvement includes interstitial lung disease and pulmonary fibrosis, with renal involvement such as lupus nephritis also represented.

phenotypes.label: Hepatitis B and spondyloarthropathy mix
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine chronic hepatitis B and related hepatobiliary findings (steatosis, gallbladder cholesterolosis/polyps). A separate cluster comprises immune-mediated rheumatologic disorders including ankylosing spondylitis, arteritis, panniculitis, and connective tissue disease, with scattered dermatologic/STD entries. These reflect two unrelated clinical themes rather than a single unified phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine neurofibromatosis type 1 with peripheral nerve tumors and female breast cancer alongside unrelated patterns such as chronic bronchitis with tobacco use and schizoaffective disorder. Additional metabolic and ocular findings appear, but there is no single unifying clinical mechanism across these conditions.

phenotypes.label: CNS tumors/pituitary disorders and exanthems
phenotypes.description: Leading conditions include meningioma and other intracranial neoplasms with pituitary disorders (hypopituitarism, diabetes insipidus), alongside narcolepsy. These are intermixed with vaccine‑preventable childhood infections (varicella, measles, mumps, rubella) and atopic disease (allergic disposition, exercise‑induced bronchospasm). The combination spans unrelated themes rather than a single clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Long COVID and personality disorders
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine post‑acute COVID‑19 with severe personality disorders (dissociative identity disorder and borderline personality disorder). These represent unrelated clinical domains, with additional generic symptom codes, so there is no single coherent clinical pattern tying them together.
